description_long: This is a round, bulbous ceramic vase with a wide body and a slightly narrowed neck, showing significant signs of age and damage. The surface is matte and dark, with visible cracks and several large missing fragments, especially prominent on one side. The vessel appears to have been reconstructed from multiple broken pieces, as evidenced by the visible seam lines and mismatched edges. There is a small, rectangular label affixed to one side, suggesting it may have been cataloged or displayed in a collection. The overall form is simple and utilitarian, lacking any decorative embellishments or handles. Its weathered appearance and incomplete structure give it an archaeological or historical character.
description: A dark, cracked ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body and several large missing fragments, showing signs of age and restoration.
description_view_0: This view highlights the largest area of breakage, with several missing pieces and jagged edges clearly visible.
description_view_1: The vase appears mostly intact from this angle, with visible crack lines but no major holes or missing sections.
description_view_2: This side shows a continuous, rounded surface with subtle crack lines and no major visible damage.
description_view_3: This view reveals both the cracked surface and a small label attached to the vase, as well as some missing fragments near the opening.

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
